rulings.Case Title: GIANCARLO MARANGHI VS. BENNY CHETCUTI JR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 26, 2012, Line 2. PLAINTIFF GIANCARLO MARANGHI'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KERRI JOHNSON VS. DTG OPERATIONS , INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Reopen Disco
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 26, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANT CAROLYN GILLESPIE BASS' Motion To Reopen Discovery. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed by Plaintiff. Motion granted in part; denied in part. The motion to completely reopen discovery is denied due to the close proximity of the trial set for June 4, 2012. However, the Court grants the motion to allow the moving party to serve subpoenas on Plaintiff's medical providers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA A. JAGIELLO VS. SUSAN ELISABETH CHRISTMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 26, 2012, Line 13, CROSS-DEFENDANT BARBARA JAGIELLO'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT - Sustained without leave to amend as to the UCL and breach of contract claims and overruled as to the conversion claim. Ms. Christman lacks standing to bring the UCL claim either individually or as a class action. Ms. Christman failed to allege her performance of the contract or excuse for nonperformance and also failed to allege that Ms. Jagiello had a contractual obligation to provide Ms. Christman's file. It does not appear that any of these deficiencies can be cured. The conversion claim is adequately pled. The request for judicial notice is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TRIBECA COMPANIES, LLC VS. FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant First American Title Insurance Company'S Demurrer To 3rd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 26, 2012, Line 15, Defendant First American Title Insurance Company'S Demurrer To 3rd Amended Complaint - Overruled as to Sky Pacific's fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action and Tribeca's seventh and eight causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to Sky Pacific's seventh and eighth causes of action. Sky Pacific has alleged sufficient facts showing it has standing to bring the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action against Defendant American Title. Sky Pacific failed to allege a misrepresentation made to it and thus its seventh and eight causes of action are deficient and it does not appear that this deficiency can be cured. Tribeca's seventh and eighth causes of action are adequately pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HEIDI TAN et al VS. PASQUALE IANNETTI ART GALLERIES,INC.,A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 26, 2012, Line 14, PLAINTIFFS HEIDI TAN, GARY SNG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The motion for summary judgment is denied as to both defendants. The motion for summary adjudication is granted against Pasquale Iannetti Art Galleries, Inc. on the causes of action for breach of contract and rescission based upon mistake and denied as to Pasquale Iannetti Art Galleries, Inc. on the cause of action for fraud and as to Mr. Pasquale Iannetti on all causes of action. Pasquale Iannetti Art Galleries, Inc. Defendants concede that the causes of action for breach of contract and rescission based upon mistake are meritorious as to Pasquale Iannetti Art Galleries, Inc. There is a triable issue regarding scienter on the fraud cause of action against both defendants. Plaintiff fails to present a basis for liability against Mr. Pasquale Iannetti on the breach of contract and rescission causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DAVID ACHTERKIRCHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Hearing On Petition For An Order Compelling Arbitreation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 26, 2012, Line 5, PETITIONER DAVID ACHTERKIRCHEN's Re-Noticed Petition For An Order Compelling Arbitration - Granted. Respondent did not timely seek mediation and Petitioner now has the right to arbitration. Per Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.6, the court nominates the following five persons, all of who are affiliated with the San Francisco office of ADR Services: 1) Ina Gyemant; 2) Everett Hewlett; 3) Michael McCabe; 4) Bonnie Sabraw; and 5) Alex Saldamando. The parties are required to send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org no later than 5pm May 3, 2012 stating whether or not they agreed on an arbitrator, and, if they did not, the court will select the arbitrator from one of the nominees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
